ip_outcome_note: Card name, art, and Kanto form confirmed as Geodude via PokemonTCG.io API (swsh8-135). Vision confirmed no magnetic markings or dark coloration, consistent with Kanto form. Internal Pokemon IP — no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Geodude (National Pokedex no. 74) is a Generation I Rock/Ground-type introduced in 1996. Official species classification: Rock Pokemon. A Kanto staple and one of the most commonly encountered wild Pokemon in early-route cave systems including Mt. Moon and Rock Tunnel.

This print is the standard Kanto form (gray body, Rock/Ground typing). The Alolan regional variant introduced in Generation VII (Sun and Moon, 2016) is Rock/Electric, with slate-gray body, magnetic black eyebrows and iron-sand hair, and three-fingered hands rather than five. Vision pass confirmed this print shows no magnetic markings, consistent with Kanto form.
sources: Bulbapedia Geodude Alolan form via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Geodude evolves into Graveler at level 25, which evolves into Golem when traded. Golem is one of the few trade-only evolutions in the original 151.
"It uses both hands to climb precipitous cliffs. People who see it in action have been known to take up bouldering."
